detect.predict.instructions: Decide whether the supplied passage was generated by an AI.
detect.predict.signature:
StringSignature(text -> reasoning, is_ai
    instructions='Decide whether the supplied passage was generated by an AI.'
    text = Field(annotation=str required=True json_schema_extra={'desc': 'Passage to classify', '__dspy_field_type': 'input', 'prefix': 'Text:'})
    reasoning = Field(annotation=str required=True json_schema_extra={'desc': '${reasoning}', '__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Reasoning:'})
    is_ai = Field(annotation=bool required=True json_schema_extra={'desc': 'True for AI-generated text; otherwise False', '__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Is Ai:'})
)

fields.name: is_ai
fields.description: True for AI-generated text; otherwise False

demos.text: These three QuerySets are separate. The first is a base ~django.db.models.query.QuerySet containing all entries that contain a headline starting with "What". The second is a subset of the first, with an additional criteria that excludes records whose pub_date is today or in the future. The third is a subset of the first, with an additional criteria that selects only the records whose pub_date is today or in the future. The initial ~django.db.models.query.QuerySet (q1) is unaffected by the refinement process.
demos.reasoning: The passage has a formal, documentation-style explanation with precise technical terminology and explicit relationships among the three QuerySets. Its wording is structured and polished, but it also resembles established framework documentation rather than newly generated conversational prose. Therefore, it is more likely human-authored or source-derived than AI-generated.

demos.text: Spark SQL supports automatically converting an RDD of JavaBeans into a DataFrame. The BeanInfo, obtained using reflection, defines the schema of the table. Currently, Spark SQL does not support JavaBeans that contain Map field(s). Nested JavaBeans and List or Array fields are supported though. You can create a JavaBean by creating a class that implements Serializable and has getters and setters for all of its fields.
